_comment: Hand-authored upstream SynthesisOutput fixture for traffic-1. Used as upstream context for the audit suite.

claimant_position.position: LTA submits the solid white line at the junction was unobscured and the camera was within calibration tolerances.

respondent_position.position: Accused submits the lane markings were obscured by roadworks signage and that the camera reading should be discounted.

contested_points.description: Whether the solid white line was visible at the moment of crossing.
contested_points.claimant_view: LTA enforcement summary asserts the line was unobscured.
contested_points.respondent_view: Driver dashcam footage purports to show obscured markings.

counter_arguments: Driver could argue procedural unfairness if calibration certificate is challenged.

preliminary_conclusion: On the documentary record, the offence under RTA s.65 is likely made out; the obscured-markings defence is weak given the calibration certificate.

reasoning_chain.description: Camera calibration certificate establishes evidentiary reliability under RTA Schedule 9.
LTA enforcement summary places the contested crossing at a junction location distinct from the obscured-markings area.

uncertainty_flags.rationale: Driver's dashcam footage has not been independently verified for the precise moment of crossing.
